“And I shall punish you according to the fruit of your deeds,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY. “And I shall kindle a fire in its forest and it shall consume all that is around her.” ’ ”
Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “Go down to the house of the sovereign of Yahuḏah. And there you shall speak this word,
and shall say, ‘Hear the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY, O sovereign of Yahuḏah, you who sit on the throne of Dawiḏ, you and your servants and your people who enter these gates!

‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “Do right- ruling and righteousness and deliver him who is robbed out of the hand of the oppressor. Do not maltreat nor do violence to the stranger, the fatherless, or the widow, nor shed innocent blood in this place.

“For if you certainly do this word, then there shall enter in by the gates of this house sovereigns sitting on the throne of Dawiḏ, riding on horses and in chariots, he and his servants and his people.
“But if you do not obey these words, I swear by Myself,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “that this house shall become a ruin.” ’ ”
For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY to the house of the sovereign of Yahuḏah, “You are like Gil‛aḏ to Me, the head of Leḇanon. But I shall make you a wilderness and cities which are not inhabited.
“And I shall prepare destroyers against you, each with his weapons. And they shall cut down your choice cedars and throw them into the fire.
“And many gentiles shall pass by this city and they shall say to one another, ‘Why has (YAHUAH) HWHY done so to this great city?’
“Then they shall say, ‘Because they have forsaken the berith of (YAHUAH) HWHY their Aluahim and bowed themselves to other mighty ones and served them.’ ”
Do not weep for the dead, nor mourn for him. Weep bitterly for him who goes away, for he shall never come back nor see the land of his birth.
For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY concerning Shallum son of Yoshiyahu, sovereign of Yahuḏah, who reigned instead of Yoshiyahu his father, who went from this place, “He shall never come back,
but he shall die in the place where they have exiled him and not see this land again.
“Woe to him who builds his house without righteousness and his rooms without right-ruling, who uses his neighbour’s service without wages and does not give him his wages,
who says, ‘I am going to build myself a wide house with spacious rooms and cut out windows for it, panelling it with cedar and painting it in red.’
“Do you reign because you enclose yourself in cedar? Did not your father eat and drink and do right-ruling and righteousness? Then it was well with him.
”He defended the cause of the poor and needy – then it was well. Was this not to know Me?” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“But your eyes and your heart are only upon your own greedy gain and on shedding innocent blood and on oppression and on doing violence.”

Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY concerning Yehoyaqim son of Yoshiyahu, sovereign of Yahuḏah, “Let them not lament for him: ‘Alas, my brother!’ or ‘Alas, my sister!’ Let them not lament for him: ‘Alas, Master!’ or ‘Alas, his excellency!’
”He shall be buried with the burial of a donkey, dragged and thrown outside the gates of Yerushalayim.
“Go up to Leḇanon and cry out and lift up your voice in Bashan. And cry from Aḇarim, for all your lovers are destroyed.
“I have spoken to you in your ease, but you said, ‘I do not listen.’ This has been your way from your youth, for you did not obey My voice.
“Let all your shepherds be devoured by the ruach and your lovers go into captivity. Then you shall be ashamed and humiliated for all your evil.
“You who dwell in Leḇanon, being nested in the cedars, how you shall groan when pangs come upon you, like the pains of a woman giving birth!
“As I live,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “though Konyahu son of Yehoyaqim, sovereign of Yahuḏah, were the signet on My right hand, I would still pull you off from there;
and I shall give you into the hand of those who seek your life and into the hand of those whose face you fear, into the hand of Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel and the hand of the Chaldeans.
“And I shall hurl you out and your mother who bore you, into another land where you were not born and there you shall die.
“But they shall not return to the land to which their being yearns to return.
“Is this man Konyahu a despised broken pot, or an undesirable vessel? Why are they hurled out, he and his descendants and cast into a land which they do not know?
“O land, land, land! Hear the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY!

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘Write this man down as childless, a strong man who is not to prosper in his days, for none of his descendants shall prosper, sitting on the throne of Dawiḏ, or rule any more in Yahuḏah.’ ”
“Woe to the shepherds destroying and scattering the sheep of My pasture!” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.

Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal against the shepherds who feed My people, “You have scattered My flock, driven them away and have not tended them. See, I am punishing you for the evil of your deeds,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“Therefore I shall gather the remnant of My flock out of all the lands where I have driven them and shall bring them back to their fold. And they shall bear and increase.
“And I shall raise up shepherds over them and they shall feed them. And they shall fear no more, nor be discouraged, nor shall they be lacking,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.

“See, the days are coming,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “when I shall raise for Dawiḏ a Branch of righteousness and a Sovereign shall reign and act wisely and shall do right-ruling and righteousness in the earth.
“In His days Yahuḏah shall be saved and Yasharal dwell safely. And this is His Name whereby He shall be called: ‘(YAHUAH) HWHY our Righteousness.’
“Therefore, see, the days are coming,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “when they shall say no more, ‘As (YAHUAH) HWHY lives who brought up the children of Yasharal out of the land of Mitsrayim,’

but, ‘As (YAHUAH) HWHY lives who brought up and led the seed of the house of Yasharal out of the land of the north and from all the lands where I had driven them.’ And they shall dwell on their own soil.”
My heart within me is broken because of the neḇi'im; all my bones shake. I am like a drunken man and like a man overcome by wine, because of (YAHUAH) HWHY and because of His set-apart words.
For the land is filled with adulterers; for the land mourns because of a curse. The pastures of the wilderness are dried up. And their course is evil and their might is not right.
“For both naḇi and kohen have become defiled. Even in My house I have found their evil,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“Therefore their way is to them like slippery ways in the dark. They are driven on and they shall fall in them. For I bring evil on them, the year of their punishment,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“And I have seen folly in the neḇi'im of Shomeron: they prophesied by Ba‛al and led My people Yasharal astray.

“And among the neḇi'im of Yerushalayim I have seen a horrible matter: committing adultery and walking in falsehood. And they strengthen the hands of evil ones, so that no one turns back from his evil. All of them are like Seḏom to Me and her inhabitants like Amorah.

“Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts concerning the neḇi'im, ‘See, I am making them eat wormwood and shall make them drink poisoned water. For defilement has gone out into all the land from the neḇi'im of Yerushalayim.’ ”
Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, “Do not listen to the words of the neḇi'im who prophesy to you, they lead you astray. They speak a vision of their own heart, not from the mouth of (YAHUAH) HWHY.

“They keep on saying to those who despise Me, ‘(YAHUAH) HWHY has said you shall have shalom.’ And to all who walk according to the stubbornness of their own heart they say, ‘No evil comes upon you.’ ”
For who has stood in the counsel of (YAHUAH) HWHY and has seen and heard His word? Who has listened to His word and obeyed it?
See, a storm of (YAHUAH) HWHY shall go forth in a rage, a whirling storm! It whirls on the head of the wrong.
The displeasure of (YAHUAH) HWHY shall not turn back until He has done and established the purposes of His heart. In the latter days you shall understand it perfectly.
“I did not send these neḇi'im, yet they ran. I have not spoken to them, yet they prophesied.
“But if they had stood in My counsel, then they would have let My people hear My Words and they would have turned them from their evil way and from the evil of their deeds.
“Am I an Aluahim close by,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “and not an Aluahim afar off?
“If anyone is hidden in secret places, would I not see him?” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY. “Do I not fill the shamayim and earth?” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“I have heard what the neḇi'im have said who prophesy falsehood in My Name, saying, ‘I have dreamed, I have dreamed!’
“Til when shall it be in the heart of the neḇi'im? – the neḇi'im of falsehood and neḇi'im of the deceit of their own heart,
who try to make My people forget My Name by their dreams which everyone relates to his neighbour, as their fathers forgot My Name for Ba‛al.
“The naḇi who has a dream, let him relate the dream and he who has My Word, let him speak My Word in truth. What is the chaff to the wheat?” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“Is not My Word like a fire?” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “and like a hammer that shatters a rock?
“Therefore see, I am against the neḇi'im,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “who steal My Words every one from his neighbour.
“See, I am against the neḇi'im,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “who use their tongues and say, ‘He declares.’

“See, I am against those who prophesy false dreams,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “and relate them and lead My people astray by their falsehoods and by their reckless boasting. But I Myself did not send them nor have I commanded them. And they do not profit this people at all,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“And when these people or the naḇi or the kohen ask you, saying, ‘What is the message of (YAHUAH) HWHY?’ then you shall say to them, ‘What message?’ I shall forsake you,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“As for the naḇi and the kohen and the people who say, ‘The message of (YAHUAH) HWHY,’ I shall punish that man and his house.
“This is what each one says to his neighbour and each one to his brother, ‘What has (YAHUAH) HWHY answered?’ and, ‘What has (YAHUAH) HWHY spoken ?’
“But the message of (YAHUAH) HWHY you no longer remember! For every man’s message is his own word, for you have changed the Words of the living Aluahim, (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, our Aluahim!
“This is what you say to the naḇi, ‘What has (YAHUAH) HWHY answered you?’ and, ‘What has (YAHUAH) HWHY spoken ?’

“But since you say, ‘The message of (YAHUAH) HWHY!’ therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘Because you say this word, “The message of (YAHUAH) HWHY!” and I have sent to you, saying, “Do not say, ‘The message of (YAHUAH) HWHY!’ ”
therefore see, I, I shall utterly forget you and cast you away from My presence, along with the city that I gave you and your fathers.
‘And I shall put an everlasting reproach on you and an everlasting shame that is not forgotten.’ ”

(YAHUAH) HWHY showed me and look, there were two baskets of figs set before the Hĕḵal of (YAHUAH) HWHY, after Neḇuḵaḏrets- tsar sovereign of Baḇel had exiled Yeḵonyahu son of Yehoyaqim, sovereign of Yahuḏah and the heads of Yahuḏah with the craftsmen and smiths, from Yerushalayim and had brought them to Baḇel.
One basket had very good figs, like the figs that are first ripe. And the other basket had very spoilt figs which could not be eaten, they were so spoilt.”
And (YAHUAH) HWHY said to me, “What do you see, Yirmeyahu?” And I said, “Figs, the good figs, very good. And the spoilt, very spoilt, which could not be eaten, they are so spoilt.”
Again the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to me, saying,

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Like these good figs, so do I acknowledge the exiles of Yahuḏah, whom I have sent out of this place for their own good, into the land of the Chaldeans.
‘And I shall set My eyes on them for good and shall bring them back to this land. I shall build them and not pull them down and shall plant them and not pluck them up.
‘And I shall give them a heart to know Me, that I am (YAHUAH) HWHY. They shall be My people and I shall be their Aluahim, for they shall turn back to Me with all their heart.

‘And as the spoilt figs that could not be eaten because they are so spoilt,’ for thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘so do I give up Tsiḏqiyahu, the sovereign of Yahuḏah, his heads, the rest of Yerushalayim who remain in this land and those who dwell in the land of Mitsrayim.
‘And I shall make them a horror to all the reigns of the earth, for evil, to be a reproach and a byword, a mockery and a curse, in all the places to which I drive them.
‘And I shall send the sword, the scarcity of food and the pestilence among them, til they are consumed from the land that I gave to them and their fathers.’ ”

The word that came to Yirmeyahu concerning all the people of Yahuḏah, in the fourth year of Yehoyaqim son of Yoshiyahu, the sovereign of Yahuḏah, which was the first year of Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel,
which Yirmeyahu the naḇi spoke to all the people of Yahuḏah and to all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, saying,

“From the thirteenth year of Yoshiyahu son of Amon, sovereign of Yahuḏah, even to this day, this is the twenty-third year in which the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY has come to me. And I have spoken to you, rising early and speaking, but you have not listened.
“Moreover, (YAHUAH) HWHY has sent to you all His servants the neḇi'im, rising early and sending them, but you have not listened nor inclined your ear to hear,
saying, ‘Turn back now everyone from his evil way and from the evil of your deeds and dwell on the soil which (YAHUAH) HWHY has given to you and your fathers forever and ever.
‘And do not go after other mighty ones to serve them and to bow down to them. Do not provoke Me with the works of your hands, so that I do you no evil.’
“But you did not listen to Me,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “so as to provoke Me with the works of your hands, for your own evil.
“Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, ‘Because you did not obey My words,

see, I am sending and taking all the tribes of the north,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘and Neḇuḵaḏretstsar the sovereign of Baḇel, My servant and shall bring them against this land and against its inhabitants and against these nations all around and shall put them under the ban and make them an astonishment and a hissing and everlasting ruins.
‘And I shall banish from them the voice of rejoicing and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the sound of the millstones and the light of the lamp.
‘And all this land shall be a ruin and a waste and these nations shall serve the sovereign of Baḇel seventy years.

‘And it shall be, when seventy years are completed, that I shall punish the sovereign of Baḇel and that nation, the land of the Chaldeans, for their crookedness,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘and shall make it everlasting ruins.
‘And I shall bring on that land all My words which I have pronounced against it, all that is written in this book, which Yirmeyahu has prophesied concerning all the nations.
‘For they too shall be enslaved by many nations and great sovereigns. And I shall repay them according to their deeds and according to the works of their hands.’ ”
For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal to me, “Take this wine cup of wrath from My hand and make all the nations, to whom I send you, drink it.
“And they shall drink and shake and go mad because of the sword that I am sending among them.”
I then took the cup from the hand of (YAHUAH) HWHY and made all the nations drink, to whom (YAHUAH) HWHY had sent me,
namely Yerushalayim and the cities of Yahuḏah and its sovereigns, its heads, to make them a ruin, an object of astonishment, a hissing and a curse, as it is this day;
Pharaoh sovereign of Mitsrayim and his servants and his heads and all his people;
and all the mixed multitude and all the sovereigns of the land of Uts and all the sovereigns of the land of the Philistines – even Ashqelon and Azzah and Eqron and the remnant of Ashdoḏ;
Eḏom and Mo’aḇ and the children of Ammon;
and all the sovereigns of Tsor and all the sovereigns of Tsiḏon and the sovereigns of the coastlands which are beyond the sea;
Deḏan and Tĕma and Buz and all who are in the farthest corners;
and all the sovereigns of Araḇia and all the sovereigns of the mixed multitude who dwell in the wilderness;
and all the sovereigns of Zimri and all the sovereigns of Ĕylam and all the sovereigns of the Medes;
and all the sovereigns of the north, far and near, one with another and all the reigns of the earth which are on the face of the ground. Also the sovereign of Shĕshaḵ shall drink after them.
“And you shall say to them, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, “Drink, be drunk and vomit! Fall and rise no more, because of the sword which I am sending among you.” ’
“And it shall be, when they refuse to take the cup from your hand to drink, then you shall say to them, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, “You shall drink!

“For look, I am beginning to bring evil upon the city which is called by My Name and should you be entirely unpunished? You are not going unpunished, for I am calling for a sword on all the inhabitants of the earth,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts.’

“And you shall prophesy against them all these words and say to them, ‘(YAHUAH) HWHY roars from on high and utters His voice from His set-apart dwelling place. He roars mightily over His fold, a shout as those who tread the grapes, against all the inhabitants of the earth.

“Tumult shall come to the ends of the earth, for (YAHUAH) HWHY has a controversy with the nations. He shall enter into mishpat with all flesh. The wrong He shall give to the sword,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.”
Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, “See, evil is going forth from nation to nation and a great whirlwind is raised up from the farthest parts of the earth.

“And in that day the slain of (YAHUAH) HWHY shall be from one end of the earth even to the other end of the earth. They shall not be lamented, or gathered, or buried, for they are dung on the face of the ground.
“Howl, you shepherds and cry! And roll yourselves in the ashes, you leaders of the flock! For the days of your slaughter and your scatterings are completed and you shall fall like a choice vessel.
“And a place of refuge shall perish from the shepherds and escape from the leaders of the flock.
“Hear the cry of the shepherds and a wailing of the leaders of the flock, for (YAHUAH) HWHY is ravaging their pasture!
“And the pastures of shalom shall be cut down because of the burning displeasure of (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“Like a lion He shall leave His hiding place, for their land shall become a waste because of the sword of oppressors and because of His burning displeasure.”
In the beginning of the reign of Yehoyaqim the son of Yoshiyahu, the sovereign of Yahuḏah, this word came from (YAHUAH) HWHY, saying,

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘Stand in the courtyard of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY and speak to all the cities of Yahuḏah, which come to bow themselves in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY, all the words that I command you to speak to them. Do not diminish a word.
‘If so be that they listen and each turn from his evil way, then I shall repent of the evil that I plan to do to them, because of the evil of their deeds.’
“And you shall say to them, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “If you do not listen to Me, to walk in My Torah which I set before you,
to listen to the words of My servants the neḇi'im I am sending you, even rising up early and sending them – though you have not listened –
then I shall make this house like Shiloh and make this city a curse to all the nations of the earth.” ’ ”
And the kohanim, the neḇi'im and all the people heard Yirmeyahu speaking these words in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY.

And it came to be, when Yirmeyahu had ended speaking all that (YAHUAH) HWHY had commanded him to speak to all the people, that the kohanim and the neḇi'im and all the people seized him, saying, “You shall certainly die!

“Why have you prophesied in the Name of (YAHUAH) HWHY, saying, ‘This house shall be like Shiloh and this city be dried up, without an inhabitant’?” And all the people were gathered against Yirmeyahu in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY.
And the heads of Yahuḏah heard this and they came up from the sovereign’s house to the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY and sat down in the entrance of the new gate of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY.
And the kohanim and the neḇi'im spoke to the heads and all the people, saying, “A death sentence for this man! For he has prophesied against this city, as you have heard with your ears.”
And Yirmeyahu spoke to all the heads and all the people, saying, “(YAHUAH) HWHY sent me to prophesy against this house and against this city with all the words you heard.
“Now, make good your ways and your deeds and obey the voice of (YAHUAH) HWHY your Aluahim, then (YAHUAH) HWHY shall relent concerning the evil He has pronounced against you.
“And I, look I am in your hand. Do with me as seems good and right to you.

“But know for certain that if you put me to death, you are bringing innocent blood on yourselves and on this city and on its inhabitants. For truly (YAHUAH) HWHY has sent me to you to speak all these words in your hearing.”
Then the heads and all the people said to the kohanim and the neḇi'im, “No death sentence for this man. For he has spoken to us in the Name of (YAHUAH) HWHY our Aluahim.”
And some of the Alders of the land rose up and spoke to all the qahal of the people, saying,

“Miḵayah of Moresheth prophesied in the days of Ḥizqiyahu the sovereign of Yahuḏah and he spoke to all the people of Yahuḏah, saying, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, “Tsiyon shall become a ploughed field and Yerushalayim be heaps and the mountain of the House like the bare hills of the forest.” ’

“Did Ḥizqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah and all Yahuḏah indeed put him to death? Did he not fear (YAHUAH) HWHY and seek the chen of (YAHUAH) HWHY? And the Master relented concerning the evil which He had pronounced against them. But we are doing great evil against ourselves.”

There was however a man who prophesied in the Name of (YAHUAH) HWHY, Uriyahu the son of Shemayahu of Qiryath Ye‛arim, who prophesied against this city and against this land according to all the words of Yirmeyahu.

And when Yehoyaqim the sovereign, with all his mighty men and all the heads, heard his words, the sovereign sought to put him to death. But Uriyahu heard of it and was afraid and fled and went to Mitsrayim.
Then Yehoyaqim the sovereign sent men to Mitsrayim: Alnathan the son of Aḵbor and some men who went with him to Mitsrayim.
And they brought Uriyahu from Mitsrayim and brought him to Yehoyaqim the sovereign, who smote him with the sword and threw his dead body into the graves of the common people.
However, the hand of Aḥiqam son of Shaphan was with Yirmeyahu, so as not to give him into the hand of the people to put him to death.
In the beginning of the reign of Yehoyaqim son of Yoshiyahu, the sovereign of Yahuḏah, this word came to Yirmeyah from (YAHUAH) HWHY, saying,
“This is what (YAHUAH) HWHY said to me, ‘Make for yourselves bands and yokes. Then you shall put them on your neck,

and shall send them to the sovereign of Eḏom and the sovereign of Mo’aḇ and the sovereign of the Ammonites and the sovereign of Tsor and the sovereign of Tsiḏon, by the hand of the mal'akim who come to Yerushalayim to Tsiḏqiyahu, the sovereign of Yahuḏah.
‘And you shall command them to say to their masters, “Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Say this to your masters,’
‘I have made the earth, the man and the beast that are on the face of the earth, by My great power and by My outstretched arm. And I shall give it to whom it seemed right in My eyes.
‘And now, I have given all these lands into the hand of Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar the sovereign of Baḇel, My servant. And the beasts of the field I have also given him to serve him.
‘And all nations shall serve him and his son and his son’s son, until the time of his land comes. And then many nations and great sovereigns shall make him serve them.

‘And it shall be, the nation and reign that do not serve Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar the sovereign of Baḇel and the one that does not put its neck under the yoke of the sovereign of Baḇel, that nation I shall punish,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘with the sword, scarcity of food and the pestilence, until I have consumed them by his hand.
‘So do not listen to your neḇi'im, or to your diviners, or to your dreamers, or to your observers of clouds, or your sorcerers, who speak to you, saying, “Do not serve the sovereign of Baḇel.”
‘For they are prophesying falsehood to you, to remove you far from your land. And I shall drive you out and you shall perish.
‘But the nations that bring their necks under the yoke of the sovereign of Baḇel and serve him, I shall leave in their own land,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘and they shall til it and dwell in it.’ ”
And I spoke to Tsiḏqiyah the sovereign of Yahuḏah according to all these words, saying, “Bring your necks under the yoke of the sovereign of Baḇel and serve him and his people and live!
“Why should you die, you and your people, by the sword, by the scarcity of food and by the pestilence, as (YAHUAH) HWHY has spoken against the nation that does not serve the sovereign of Baḇel?
“Do not listen to the words of the neḇi'im who speak to you, saying, ‘Do not serve the sovereign of Baḇel,’ for they prophesy falsehood to you,
for I have not sent them,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “yet they prophesy falsehood in My Name, in order that I drive you out. Then you shall perish, you and the neḇi'im who prophesy to you.”

Then I spoke to the kohanim and to all this people, saying, “Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘Do not listen to the words of your neḇi'im who are prophesying to you, saying, “See, the vessels of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY are brought back from Baḇel soon now,” for they prophesy falsehood to you.
‘Do not listen to them. Serve the sovereign of Baḇel and live! Why should this city become a ruin?

‘B u t i f th e y a r e p r o p h e t s a n d i f t h e W o rd o f H W H Y i s w i th th e m , let t h e m now make intercession to (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, that the vessels which are left in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY and in the house of the sovereign of Yahuḏah and at Yerushalayim, shall not go to Baḇel.’
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts concerning the columns and concerning the Sea and concerning the stands and concerning the remainder of the vessels which are left in this city,

which Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar the sovereign of Baḇel did not take, when he exiled Yeḵonyah the son of Yehoyaqim, the sovereign of Yahuḏah, from Yerushalayim to Baḇel and all the nobles of Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim,
thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, concerning the vessels that remain in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY and in the house of the sovereign of Yahuḏah and of Yerushalayim:
‘They shall be brought to Baḇel and be there until the day that I visit them,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY. ‘Then I shall bring them back and restore them to this place.’ ”

And it came to be in that year, at the beginning of the reign of Tsiḏqiyah sovereign of Yahuḏah, in the fourth year and in the fifth month, that Ḥananyah son of Azzur the naḇi, who was from Giḇ‛on, spoke to me in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY in the presence of the kohanim and of all the people, saying,
“Thus spoke (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, saying, ‘I have broken the yoke of the sovereign of Baḇel.
‘Within two years I am going to bring back to this place all the vessels of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY, that Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar sovereign of Baḇel removed from this place and took to Baḇel.

‘And I am going to bring back to this place Yeḵonyah son of Yehoyaqim, sovereign of Yahuḏah, with all the exiles of Yahuḏah who went to Baḇel,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘for I am going to break the yoke of the sovereign of Baḇel.’ ”
Then the naḇi Yirmeyah spoke to the naḇi Ḥananyah in the presence of the kohanim and in the presence of all the people who stood in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY,

and the naḇi Yirmeyah said, “Amĕn! (YAHUAH) HWHY do so! (YAHUAH) HWHY establish your words which you have prophesied, to bring back the vessels of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY and all the exiles from Baḇel to this place!
“Only, hear now this word that I speak in your hearing and in the hearing of all the people:
“The neḇi'im who have been before me and before you of old prophesied against many lands and great reigns, of battle and of evil and of pestilence.
“The naḇi who prophesies of shalom – when the Word of the naḇi comes to pass, that naḇi is known as one whom (YAHUAH) HWHY has truly sent.”
And Ḥananyah the naḇi took the yoke off the naḇi Yirmeyah’s neck and broke it.

And Ḥananyah spoke in the presence of all the people, saying, “Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘So I shall break the yoke of Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar sovereign of Baḇel from the neck of all nations within two years.’ ” And the naḇi Yirmeyah went his way.
And the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyah, after Ḥananyah the naḇi had broken the yoke from the neck of the naḇi Yirmeyah, saying,
“Go and speak to Ḥananyah, saying, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “You have broken the yokes of wood, but you shall make yokes of iron instead.”

‘For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, “I have put a yoke of iron on the neck of all these nations, to serve Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar sovereign of Baḇel. And they shall serve him. And I have also given him the beasts of the field.” ’ ”
And the naḇi Yirmeyah said to Ḥananyah the naḇi, “Listen, please, Ḥananyah, (YAHUAH) HWHY has not sent you, but you have made this people trust in falsehood.
“Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘See, I am sending you away from the face of the earth. This year you shall die, for you have spoken apostasy against (YAHUAH) HWHY.’ ”
And Ḥananyah the naḇi died the same year, in the seventh month.

And these are the words of the letter which Yirmeyah the naḇi sent from Yerushalayim to the rest of the Alders of the exile and to the kohanim and to the neḇi'im and to all the people whom Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar had exiled from Yerushalayim to Baḇel –
after Yeḵonyah the sovereign and the sovereigness mother and the eunuchs and the heads of Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim and the craftsmen and the smiths had gone into exile from Yerushalayim –
by the hand of Al‛asah son of Shaphan and Gemaryah the son of Ḥilqiyah, whom Tsiḏqiyah sovereign of Yahuḏah sent to Baḇel, to Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar the sovereign of Baḇel, saying,
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, Aluahim of Yasharal, to all the exiles whom I exiled from Yerushalayim to Baḇel,
‘Build houses and dwell in them, plant gardens and eat their fruit.
‘Take wives and bring forth sons and daughters. And take wives for your sons and give your daughters to husbands and let them bear sons and daughters and be increased there and not diminished.
‘And seek the shalom of the city where I have exiled you and pray to (YAHUAH) HWHY for it, for in its shalom you have shalom.’
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Let not your neḇi'im and your diviners who are in your midst deceive you, neither listen to the dreams which you are dreaming.
‘For they are prophesying falsely to you in My Name. I have not sent them,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘When seventy years are completed, at Baḇel I shall visit you and establish My good word toward you, to bring you back to this place.
‘For I know the plans I am planning for you,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘plans of shalom and not of evil, to give you a future and an expectancy.
‘Then you shall call on Me and shall come and pray to Me and I shall listen to you.
‘And you shall seek Me and shall find Me, when you search for Me with all your heart.

‘And I shall be found by you,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘and I shall turn back your captivity and shall gather you from all the gentiles and from all the places where I have driven you, declares (YAHUAH) HWHY. And I shall bring you back to the place from which I have exiled you.’
“Because you have said, ‘(YAHUAH) HWHY has raised up neḇi'im for us in Baḇel’ –

thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY concerning the sovereign who sits on the throne of Dawiḏ, concerning all the people who dwell in this city and concerning your brothers who have not gone out with you into exile,
thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, ‘See, I am sending on them the sword, the scarcity of food and the pestilence. And I shall make them like spoilt figs, so spoilt as to be uneatable.

‘And I shall pursue them with the sword, with scarcity of food and with pestilence. And I shall make them a horror among all the reigns of the earth, to be a curse and an astonishment and a hissing and a reproach among all the gentiles where I have driven them.
‘For they did not heed My words,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘which I sent to them by My servants the neḇi'im, rising up early and sending them, yet you did not listen,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“You, therefore, hear the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY, all you exiles whom I have sent from Yerushalayim to Baḇel.

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, concerning Aḥaḇ son of Qolayah and Tsiḏqiyahu son of Ma‛asĕyah, who are prophesying falsely to you in My Name, ‘See, I am giving them into the hand of Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel and he shall smite them before your eyes.
‘And because of them all the exiles of Yahuḏah who are in Baḇel shall use a curse, saying, “(YAHUAH) HWHY make you like Tsiḏqiyahu and Aḥaḇ, whom the sovereign of Baḇel roasted in the fire,”

because they have done wickedness in Yasharal and committed adultery with their neighbours’ wives and have spoken a word in My Name falsely which I have not commanded them. And I am He who knows and a witness,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“And speak to Shemayahu the Neḥelamite, saying,

“Thus speaks (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, saying, ‘Because you have sent letters in your name to all the people who are at Yerushalayim, to Tsephanyah son of Ma‛asĕyah the kohen and to all the kohanim, saying,

“(YAHUAH) HWHY has made you kohen instead of Yehoyaḏa the kohen, so that there are overseers in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY over everyone who is mad and makes himself a naḇi, that you should put him in the stocks and in the iron collar.
“So why have you not reproved Yirmeyahu of Anathoth who makes himself a naḇi to you?
“For he has sent to us in Baḇel, saying, ‘This captivity is long – build houses and dwell in them and plant gardens and eat their fruit.’ ” ’ ”
And Tsephanyah the kohen read this letter in the hearing of Yirmeyahu the naḇi.
Then the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu, saying,

“Send to all those in exile, saying, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY concerning Shemayah the Neḥelamite, “Because Shemayah has prophesied to you and I have not sent him and he has made you to trust on falsehood,”

therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “See, I am bringing punishment upon Shemayah the Neḥelamite and his seed: he shall have no one to dwell among this people, nor is he to see the good that I am about to do for My people,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “because he has spoken apostasy against (YAHUAH) HWHY.” ’ ”
The word that came to Yirmeyahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY, saying,
“Thus spoke (YAHUAH) HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal, saying, ‘Write in a book for yourself all the words that I have spoken to you.

‘For look, the days are coming,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘when I shall turn back the captivity of My people Yasharal and Yahuḏah,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘and I shall bring them back to the land that I gave to their fathers and let them possess it.’ ”
And these are the words (YAHUAH) HWHY spoke concerning Yasharal and Yahuḏah.
“For this is what (YAHUAH) HWHY said, ‘We have heard a voice of trembling, of fear and not of shalom.
‘Ask now and see if a man is giving birth. Why do I see every man with his hands on his loins like a woman in labour and all faces turned pale?
‘Oh! For great is that day, there is none like it. And it is the time of Ya‛aqoḇ’s distress, but he shall be saved out of it.
‘And it shall be in that day,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, ‘that I break his yoke from your neck and tear off your bonds and foreigners no more enslave them.
‘And they shall serve (YAHUAH) HWHY their Aluahim and Dawiḏ their sovereign, whom I raise up for them.

‘And you, do not fear, O Ya‛aqoḇ My servant,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘nor be discouraged, O Yasharal. For look, I am saving you from afar and your seed from the land of their captivity. And Ya‛aqoḇ shall return and have rest and be at ease, with no one to trouble him.

‘For I am with you,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘to save you. Though I make a complete end of all gentiles where I have scattered you, yet I do not make a complete end of you. But I shall reprove you in mishpat and by no means leave you unpunished.’
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘Your breach is incurable, your wound is grievous.
‘No one pleads your cause, to bind up. There are no healing medicines for you.
‘All those loving you have forgotten you, they do not seek you. For I smote you as an enemy smites, with cruel chastisement because your crookedness is great, your sins have increased.
‘Why do you cry about your breach? Your pain is incurable. Because of your many crookednesses, because your sins have increased, I have done this to you.

‘However, all those who devour you shall be devoured. And all your adversaries, every one of them, shall go into captivity. And those who exploit you shall be exploited and all who prey upon you I shall make a prey.
‘For I restore health to you and heal you of your wounds,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘for they have called you an outcast saying, “This is Tsiyon, no one is seeking her.” ’

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘See, I turn back the captivity of Ya‛aqoḇ’s tents and have compassion on his dwelling places. And the city shall be built upon its own mound and the palace stand on its right place.
‘And out of them shall arise thanksgiving and the voice of those who are laughing. And I shall increase them and they shall not diminish. And I shall esteem them and they shall not be small.
‘And his children shall be as before and his qahal shall be established before Me. And I shall punish all who oppress them.

‘And his Prince shall be from him and his Ruler shall come from among him. I shall bring him near and he shall approach Me, for who is this who pledged his heart to approach Me?’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
‘And you shall be My people and I shall be your Aluahim.’ ”
See, the storm of (YAHUAH) HWHY shall go forth in a rage, a whirling storm! It bursts upon the head of the wrong.
The burning displeasure of (YAHUAH) HWHY shall not turn back until He has done and established the purposes of His heart. In the latter days you shall understand it.
“At that time, ” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “I shall be the Aluahim of all the clans of Yasharal and they shall be My people.”
Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “A people escaped from the sword found chen in the wilderness, Yasharal, when it went to find rest.”
(YAHUAH) HWHY appeared to me from afar, saying, “I have loved you with an everlasting love, therefore I shall draw you with kindness.
“I am going to build you again. And you shall be rebuilt, O maiden of Yasharal! Again you shall take up your tambourines and go forth in the dances of those who rejoice.
“Again you shall plant vines on the mountains of Shomeron. The planters shall plant and treat them as common.
“For there shall be a day when the watchmen cry on Mount Ephrayim, ‘Arise and let us go up to Tsiyon, to (YAHUAH) HWHY our Aluahim.’ ”
For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “Sing with gladness for Ya‛aqoḇ and shout among the chief of the nations. Cry out, give praise and say, ‘O (YAHUAH) HWHY, save Your people, the remnant of Yasharal!’

“See, I am bringing them from the land of the north and shall gather them from the ends of the earth, among them the blind and the lame, those with child and those in labour, together – a great qahal returning here.

“With weeping they shall come and with their prayers I bring them. I shall make them walk by rivers of waters, in a straight way in which they do not stumble. For I shall be a Father to Yasharal and Ephrayim – he is My first- born.
“Hear the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY, O gentiles and declare it in the isles afar off and say, ‘He who scattered Yasharal gathers him and shall guard him as a shepherd his flock.’
“For (YAHUAH) HWHY shall ransom Ya‛aqoḇ and redeem him from the hand of one stronger than he.

“And they shall come in and shall sing on the height of Tsiyon and stream to the goodness of (YAHUAH) HWHY, for grain and for new wine and for oil and for the young of the flock and the herd. And their being shall be like a well-watered garden and never languish again.
“Then shall a maiden rejoice in a dance and young men and old, together. And I shall turn their mourning to joy and shall comfort them and shall make them rejoice from their sorrow,
and shall fill the being of the kohanim with fatness. And My people shall be satisfied with My goodness,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “A voice was heard in Ramah, wailing, bitter weeping, Raḥĕl weeping for her children, refusing to be comforted for her children, because they are no more.”
Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “Hold back your voice from weeping and your eyes from tears, for there is a reward for your work,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “and they shall return from the land of the enemy.
“And there is expectancy for your latter end,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “and your children shall return to their own country.
“I have clearly heard Ephrayim lamenting, ‘You have chastised me and I was chastised, like an untrained calf. Turn me back and I shall turn back, for You are (YAHUAH) HWHY my Aluahim.
‘For after my turning back, I repented. And after I was instructed, I struck myself on the thigh. I was ashamed, even humiliated, for I bore the reproach of my youth.’

“Is Ephrayim a precious son to Me, a child of delights? For though I spoke against him, I still remembered him. That is why My affections were deeply moved for him. I have great compassion for him,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“Set up signposts, make landmarks; set your heart toward the highway, the way in which you went. Turn back, O maiden of Yasharal, turn back to these cities of yours!
“Til when would you turn here and there, O backsliding daughter? For (YAHUAH) HWHY has created what is new on earth: a woman encompasses a man!”

Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, “Let them once again say this word in the land of Yahuḏah and in its cities, when I turn back their captivity, ‘(YAHUAH) HWHY bless you, O home of righteousness, mountain of set-apartness!’
“And in Yahuḏah and all its cities farmers and those who journey with flocks, shall dwell together.
“For I shall fill the weary being and I shall replenish every grieved being.”
At this I awoke and looked around and my sleep was sweet to me.
“See, the days are coming,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “that I shall sow the house of Yasharal and the house of Yahuḏah with the seed of man and the seed of beast.
“And it shall be, that as I have watched over them to pluck up and to break down, to throw down, to destroy and to afflict, so I shall watch over them to build and to plant,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“In those days they shall no longer say, ‘The fathers ate sour grapes and the children’s teeth are blunted.’
“But each one shall die for his own crookedness – whoever eats sour grapes, his teeth shall be blunted.
“See, the days are coming,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “when I shall make a new berith with the house of Yasharal and with the house of Yahuḏah,

not like the berith I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Mitsrayim, My berith which they broke, though I was a husband to them,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.

“For this is the berith I shall make with the house of Yasharal after those days, declares (YAHUAH) HWHY: I shall put My Torah in their inward parts and write it on their hearts. I shall be their Aluahim and they shall be My people.

“And no longer shall they teach, each one his neighbour and each one his brother, saying, ‘Know (YAHUAH) HWHY,’ for they shall all know Me, from the least of them to the greatest of them,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY. “For I shall forgive their crookedness and remember their sin no more.”

Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, who gives the sun for a light by day and the laws of the moon and the stars for a light by night, who stirs up the sea and its waves roar – (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts is His Name:
“If these laws vanish from before Me,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “then the seed of Yasharal shall also cease from being a nation before Me forever.”

Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “If the shamayim above could be measured and the foundations of the earth searched out beneath, I would also cast off all the seed of Yasharal for all that they have done,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“See, the days are coming,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “that the city shall be built for (YAHUAH) HWHY from the Tower of Ḥanan’Al to the Corner Gate.
“And the measuring line shall again extend straight ahead to the hill Garĕḇ, then it shall turn toward Go‛ah.

“And all the valley of the dead bodies and of the ashes and all the fields as far as the wadi Qiḏron, to the corner of the Horse Gate toward the east, is to be set- apart to (YAHUAH) HWHY. It shall not be plucked up or thrown down any more forever.”
The word that came to Yirmeyahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY in the tenth year of Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah, which was the eighteenth year of Neḇuḵaḏretstsar.
Now at that time the army of the sovereign of Baḇel besieged Yerushalayim and Yirmeyahu the naḇi was shut up in the court of the guard, which was in the house of the sovereign of Yahuḏah.

For Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah had shut him up, saying, “Why are you prophesying, saying, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “See, I am giving this city into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel and he shall take it,

and Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah shall not escape from the hand of the Chaldeans, but certainly be given into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel and shall speak with him face to face and see him eye to eye,
and he shall lead Tsiḏqiyahu to Baḇel and be there until I visit him,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, “though you fight with the Chaldeans, you shall not prosper” ’?”
And Yirmeyahu said, “The Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to me, saying,
‘See, Ḥaname’Al son of Shallum your uncle is coming to you, saying, “Buy my field which is in Anathoth, for the right of redemption is yours to buy it.” ’

“So Ḥaname’Al my uncle’s son came to me in the court of the guard according to the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY and said to me, ‘Please buy my field that is in Anathoth, which is in the land of Binyamin, for the right of inheritance is yours and the redemption. Buy it for yourself.’ And I knew that this was the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“And I bought the field which was at Anathoth from Ḥaname’Al, my uncle’s son and weighed out to him the silver, seventeen sheqels of silver.
“And I signed the deed and sealed it, took witnesses and weighed the silver in the scales.
“Then I took the deed of purchase – that which was sealed according to the command and law, and that which was open –

and I gave the deed of purchase to Baruḵ son of Nĕriyah, son of Maḥsĕyah, in the presence of Ḥaname’Al my uncle’s son and in the presence of the witnesses who signed the deed of purchase, before all the Yahuḏim who sat in the court of the guard.
“And I commanded Baruḵ before their eyes, saying,

‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, “Take these deeds, both this deed of purchase which is sealed and this deed which is open and put them in an earthen vessel, so that they remain many days.”
‘For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, “Houses and fields and vineyards shall again be bought in this land.” ’
“And after I had given the deed of purchase to Baruḵ son of Nĕriyah, I prayed to (YAHUAH) HWHY, saying,
‘Ah, Master (YAHUAH) HWHY! See, You have made the shamayim and the earth by Your great power and outstretched arm. There is no matter too hard for You,
who show kindness to thousands and repay the crookedness of the fathers into the bosom of their children after them – the Great, the Mighty Al, (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts is His Name,
great in counsel and mighty in work, for Your eyes are open to all the ways of the sons of men, to give everyone according to his ways and according to the fruit of his deeds.
‘For You have set signs and wonders in the land of Mitsrayim, to this day and in Yasharal and among other men. And You have made Yourself a Name, as it is this day.
‘And You have brought Your people Yasharal out of the land of Mitsrayim with signs and wonders, with a strong hand and an outstretched arm and with great fearsome deeds.
‘And You gave them this land, of which You swore to their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.
‘And they came in and possessed it, but they did not obey Your voice nor did they walk in Your Torah. They did not do all that You commanded them to do, so You brought all this evil upon them.

‘See the siege mounds! They have come to the city to take it. And the city has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans who fight against it, because of the sword and the scarcity of food and the pestilence. And what You have spoken has come about and look, You see it!
‘Yet You, O Master (YAHUAH) HWHY, have said to me, “Buy the field for silver and take witnesses”! although the city has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans.’ ”
Then the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu, saying,
“See, I am (YAHUAH) HWHY, the Aluahim of all flesh. Is there any matter too hard for Me?”
Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “See, I am giving this city into the hand of the Chaldeans, into the hand of Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel and he shall take it.

“And the Chaldeans who fight against this city shall come and set fire to this city and burn it, with the houses on whose roofs they burned incense to Ba‛al and poured out drink offerings to other mighty ones, to provoke Me.

“For the children of Yasharal and the children of Yahuḏah have done only evil before Me, from their youth. For the children of Yasharal have only provoked Me with the work of their hands,” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
“For this city has been a cause for My displeasure and My wrath from the day that they built it, even to this day that I should remove it from before My face,

because of all the evil of the children of Yasharal and the children of Yahuḏah, which they have done to provoke Me – they, their sovereigns, their heads, their kohanim, their neḇi'im and the men of Yahuḏah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.
“And they have turned their back to Me and not their face – though I taught them, rising up early and teaching them, they did not listen to receive instruction.
“And they set their abominations in the house which is called by My Name, to defile it.

“And they built the high places of Ba‛al which are in the Valley of the son of Hinnom, to offer up their sons and their daughters to Moleḵ, which I did not command them, nor did it come into My heart, that they should do this abomination, to make Yahuḏah sin.

“And now, thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, the Aluahim of Yasharal, concerning this city of which you say, ‘It shall be given into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel by the sword and by scarcity of food and by pestilence’:
‘See, I am gathering them out of all the lands where I have driven them in My displeasure, in My wrath and in great rage. And I shall bring them back to this place and shall let them dwell in safety.
‘And they shall be My people and I shall be their Aluahim.
‘And I shall give them one heart and one way, to fear Me all the days, for the good of them and of their children after them.
‘And I shall make an everlasting berith with them, that I do not turn back from doing good to them. I shall put My fear in their hearts so as not to turn aside from Me.
‘And I shall rejoice over them to do good to them and shall plant them in this land in truth with all My heart and with all My being.’
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘As I have brought all this great evil on this people, so I am bringing on them all the good that I am speaking to them.
‘And fields shall be bought in this land of which you are saying, “It is a wasteland, without man or beast. It has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans.”

‘Fields shall be bought for silver and deeds signed and sealed and witnesses be called, in the land of Binyamin and in the places around Yerushalayim and in the cities of Yahuḏah and in the cities of the mountains and in the cities of the low country and in the cities of the South. For I shall turn back their captivity,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.”
And the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu a second time, while he was still shut up in the court of the guard, saying,
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY who made it, (YAHUAH) HWHY who formed it to establish it, (YAHUAH) HWHY is His Name,
‘Call unto Me and I shall answer you and show you great and inaccessible matters, which you have not known.’
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, the Aluahim of Yasharal, concerning the houses of this city and the houses of the sovereigns of Yahuḏah, which are thrown down against the siege mounds and the sword,
while coming to fight with the Chaldeans and to fill their places with the corpses of men whom I shall slay in My displeasure and My wrath, all for whose evil I have hidden My face from this city.
‘See, I am bringing to it relief and healing. I shall heal them and reveal to them the riches of shalom and truth.
‘And I shall turn back the captivity of Yahuḏah and the captivity of Yasharal and shall build them as at the first,
and shall cleanse them from all their crookedness that they have sinned against Me. And I shall pardon all their crookednesses that they have sinned and by which they have transgressed against Me.

‘And it shall be to Me a name of joy, a praise and a pride before all nations of the earth, who hear all the good I am doing to them and they shall fear and tremble for all the goodness and all the p e a c e I a m d o in g to i t .’ 1

the voice of joy and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the voice of those who are saying, “Praise (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, for (YAHUAH) HWHY is good, for His kindness is forever,” of those who are bringing the offering of praise into the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY. For I shall turn back the captivity of the land, as at the first,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, ‘In this place which is dried up, without man and without beast and in all its cities, there shall once again be a home of shepherds causing their flocks to lie down.

‘In the cities of the mountains, in the cities of the low country and in the cities of the South and in the land of Binyamin and in the places around Yerushalayim and in the cities of Yahuḏah, the flocks once again pass under the hands of him who counts them,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
‘See, the days are coming,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘when I shall establish the good word which I have promised to the house of Yasharal and to the house of Yahuḏah:
‘In those days and at that time I cause a Branch of righteousness to spring forth for Dawiḏ. He shall do right-ruling and righteousness in the earth.
‘In those days Yahuḏah shall be saved and Yerushalayim dwell in safety. And this is that which shall be proclaimed to her: ‘(YAHUAH) HWHY our Righteousness.’
“For thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘For Dawiḏ there is not to cease a man to sit on the throne of the house of Yasharal.
‘And for the kohanim, the Lĕwites, there is not to cease a man to offer burnt offer ings before Me, to kindle grain offerings and to slaughter continually.’ ”
And the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu, saying,
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘If you could break My berith with the day and My berith with the night, so that there be not day and night in their season,
then My berith could also be broken with Dawiḏ My servant – so that he shall not have a son to reign upon his throne – and with the Lĕwites, the kohanim, My attendants.
‘As the host of the shamayim is not counted, nor the sand of the sea measured, so I increase the descendants of Dawiḏ My servant and the Lĕwites who attend upon Me.’ ”
And the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu, saying,

“Have you not observed what these people have spoken , saying, ‘The two clans which (YAHUAH) HWHY has chosen have been rejected by Him’? So they have despised My people, no more to be a nation before them.
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘If My berith is not with day and night and if I have not appointed the laws of the shamayim and earth,

then I would also reject the descendants of Ya‛aqoḇ and Dawiḏ My servant, so that I should not take of his descendants to be rulers over the descendants of Aḇrahim, Yitsḥaq and Ya‛aqoḇ. For I shall turn back their captivity and have compassion on them.’ ”

The word which came to Yirmeyahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY, when Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar sovereign of Baḇel and all his army and all the reigns of the earth under his rule and all the people, fought against Yerushalayim and all its cities, saying,

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Go and speak to Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah and say to him, “Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘See, I am giving this city into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel. And he shall burn it with fire,

and you shall not escape out of his hand, but certainly be taken and given into his hand. And your eyes shall see the eyes of the sovereign of Baḇel and his mouth shall speak with your mouth and you shall go to Baḇel.’ ” ’
“But hear the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY, O Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah! Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY concerning you, ‘You shall not die by the sword.

‘In shalom you are to die and as in the burnings of spices for your fathers, the former sovereigns who were before you, so they shall burn spices for you and lament for you, saying, “Alas, Master!” ’ For I have spoken the word, ” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.
And Yirmeyahu the naḇi spoke all these words to Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign of Yahuḏah in Yerushalayim,

while the sovereign of Baḇel’s army was fighting against Yerushalayim and all the cities of Yahuḏah that were left, against Laḵish and Azĕqah. For only these walled cities remained of the cities of Yahuḏah.
The word which came to Yirmeyahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY, after Sovereign Tsiḏqiyahu had made a berith with all the people who were at Yerushalayim to proclaim release to them:
that everyone was to set free his male and female slave, the Heḇrew man and the Heḇrew woman, no one was to keep a Yehuḏite, his brother, enslaved.

And when all the heads and all the people who had come into the berith heard that each one was to set free his male and female slaves and not keep them enslaved any longer, they obeyed and released them.
But afterward they changed their minds and made the male and female slaves return, whom they had set free and brought them into subjection as male and female slaves.
Therefore the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY, saying,
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘I Myself made a berith with your fathers in the day that I brought them out of the land of Mitsrayim, out of the house of bondage, saying,

“At the end of seven years each one should set free his Heḇrew brother, who has been sold to him. And when he has served you six years, you shall let him go free from you.” But your fathers did not obey Me nor incline their ear.
‘And you recently turned and did what was right in My eyes, each man proclaiming release to his neighbour. And you made a berith before Me in the house which is called by My Name.

‘But you turned back and profaned My Name and each one of you took back his male and female slaves, whom he had set free, at their pleasure and brought them into subjection, to be your male and female slaves.’

“Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘You have not obeyed Me in proclaiming release, each one to his brother and each one to his neighbour. See, I am proclaiming release to you,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘to the sword, to the pestilence and to the scarcity of food! I shall make you a horror to all reigns of the earth.

‘And I shall give the men who are transgressing My berith, who have not established the words of the berith which they made before Me, when they cut the calf in two and passed between the parts of it:
the heads of Yahuḏah and the heads of Yerushalayim, the eunuchs and the kohanim and all the people of the land who passed between the parts of the calf.
‘And I shall give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life. And their corpses shall be for food to the birds of the shamayim and the beasts of the earth.

‘And I shall give Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah and his heads into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life and into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel’s army that has withdrawn from you.

‘See, I am commanding,’ declares (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘and shall bring them back to this city and they shall fight against it and take it and burn it with fire. And I shall make the cities of Yahuḏah a ruin without inhabitant.’ ”
The word which came to Yirme yahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY in the days of Yehoyaqim son of Yoshiyahu, sovereign of Yahuḏah, saying,
“Go to the house of the Rĕḵaḇites. And you shall speak to them and bring them into the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY, into one of the rooms and give them wine to drink.”
And I took Ya‛azanyah the son of Yirmeyahu, the son of Ḥaḇatstsinyah and his brothers and all his sons and all the house of the Rĕḵaḇites,

and brought them into the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY, into the room of the sons of Ḥanan son of Yiḡdalyahu, a man of Aluahim, which was by the chamber of the heads, above the room of Ma‛asĕyahu son of Shallum, the keeper of the door.
And I set before the sons of the house of the Rĕḵaḇites bowls filled with wine and cups and I said to them, “Drink wine.”
But they said, “We do not drink wine, because Yonaḏaḇ the son of Rĕḵaḇ, our father, commanded us, saying, ‘You shall not drink wine, neither you nor your sons, forever.

‘And do not build a house, neither sow seed, nor plant a vineyard, nor have any of these, but dwell in tents all your days, so that you live many days on the face of the land where you are sojourners.’
“So we obeyed the voice of Yonaḏaḇ son of Rĕḵaḇ, our father, in all that he commanded us, to drink no wine all our days, we, our wives, our sons and our daughters,
nor to build ourselves houses to dwell in. And we have no vineyard, field or seed.
“But we dwell in tents and have obeyed and done according to all that Yonaḏaḇ our father commanded us.

“And it came to be, when Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel came up into the land, that we said, ‘Come, let us go to Yerushalayim for fear of the army of the Chaldeans and for fear of the army of the Arameans.’ So we dwell at Yerushalayim.”
And the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu, saying,

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Go and you shall say to the men of Yahuḏah and to the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, “Have you not received instruction, to obey My words?” declares (YAHUAH) HWHY.

“The words of Yonaḏaḇ son of Rĕḵaḇ, which he commanded his sons, not to drink wine, are established and they have not drunk unto this day, for they have obeyed their father’s command. And as for Me, I have spoken to you, rising early and speaking, but you have not obeyed Me.

“And I sent to you all My servants the neḇi'im, rising up early and sending them, saying, ‘Turn, each one from his evil way and make good your deeds and do not go after other mighty ones to serve them and you shall dwell in the land which I have given you and your fathers.’ But you have not inclined your ear nor obeyed Me.
“The sons of Yonaḏaḇ son of Rĕḵaḇ have indeed carried out the command of their father which he commanded them, but this people has not obeyed Me.” ’

“Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY Aluahim of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘See, I am bringing on Yahuḏah and on all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim all the evil I have pronounced against them because I have spoken to them but they did not listen and I have called to them but they did not answer.’ ”

And Yirmeyahu said to the house of the Rĕḵaḇites, “Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Because you have obeyed the command of Yonaḏaḇ your father and guarded all his commands and done according to all that he commanded you,
therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, “Of Yonaḏaḇ son of Rĕḵaḇ there shall never cease to be a man to stand before Me.” ’ ”
And it came to be in the fourth year of Yehoyaqim son of Yoshiyahu, sovereign of Yahuḏah, that this word came to Yirmeyahu from (YAHUAH) HWHY, say ing,

“Take a scroll and write on it all the words that I have spoken to you against Yasharal and against Yahuḏah and against all the nations, from the day I spoke to you, from the days of Yoshiyahu even to this day.
“It could be that the house of Yahuḏah hears of all the evil which I plan to bring upon them, so that each one turns back from his evil way and I shall pardon their crookedness and their sin.”
And Yirmeyahu called Baruḵ son of Nĕriyah. And Baruḵ wrote on a scroll from the mouth of Yirmeyahu, all the words of (YAHUAH) HWHY which He had spoken to him.
And Yirmeyahu commanded Baruḵ, saying, “I am shut up, I am unable to enter the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY.

“But you shall enter and shall read from the scroll which you have written from my mouth, the words of (YAHUAH) HWHY, in the hearing of the people in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY on the day of fasting. And also read them in the hearing of all Yahuḏah who come from their cities.

“It could be that they present their supplication before (YAHUAH) HWHY and they turn back, each one from his evil way. For great is the displeasure and the wrath that (YAHUAH) HWHY has spoken against this people.”
And Baruḵ son of Nĕriyah did according to all that Yirmeyahu the naḇi commanded him, reading from the book the words of (YAHUAH) HWHY in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY.

And it came to be in the fifth year of Yehoyaqim son of Yoshiyahu, sovereign of Yahuḏah, in the ninth month, that they called a fast before (YAHUAH) HWHY to all the people in Yerushalayim and to all the people who came from the cities of Yahuḏah to Yerushalayim.

And Baruḵ read from the book the words of Yirmeyahu in the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY, in the room of Gemaryahu son of Shaphan the scribe, in the upper courtyard at the entry of the New Gate of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY, in the hearing of all the people.
And Miḵayahu son of Gemaryahu, son of Shaphan, heard all the words of (YAHUAH) HWHY from the book,

And then he went down to the sovereign’s house, into the scribe’s room. And there all the heads were sitting: Alishama the scribe and Delayahu son of Shemayahu and Alnathan son of Aḵbor and Gemaryahu son of Shaphan and Tsiḏqiyahu son of Ḥananyahu and all the heads.
And Miḵayehu told them all the words which he heard when Baruḵ read the book in the hearing of the people.

Then all the heads sent Yehuḏi son of Nethanyahu, son of Shelemyahu, son of Kushi, to Baruḵ, saying, “Take in your hand the scroll from which you have read in the hearing of the people and come.” So Baruḵ son of Nĕriyahu took the scroll in his hand and came to them.
And they said to him, “Sit down, please and read it in our hearing.” Then Baruḵ read it in their hearing.
And it came to be, when they had heard all the words, that they looked at each other in fear and said to Baruḵ, “We are certainly going to report all these words to the sovereign.”
And they asked Baruḵ, saying, “Please explain to us, how did you write all these words? From his mouth?”
And Baruḵ answered them, “From his mouth he spoke all these words to me and I wrote them with ink in the book.”
Then the heads said to Baruḵ, “Go, hide, you and Yirmeyahu and let no one know where you are.”
And they went to the sovereign, into the court. But they put the scroll in the room of Alishama the scribe and told all the words in the hearing of the sovereign.

The sovereign therefore sent Yehuḏi to bring the scroll and he took it from the room of Alishama the scribe. And Yehuḏi read it in the hearing of the sovereign and in the hearing of all the heads standing beside the sovereign.
And the sovereign was sitting in the winter house in the ninth month, with a fire burning on the hearth before him.

Then it came to be, when Yehuḏi had read three or four columns, that the sovereign cut it with the scribe’s knife and threw it into the fire that was on the hearth, until the entire scroll was burned in the fire that was on the hearth.
Yet the sovereign and all his servants who heard all these words were not afraid, nor did they tear their garments.
Moreover, Alnathan and Delayahu and Gemaryahu pleaded with the sovereign not to burn the scroll, but he did not listen to them.

And the sovereign commanded Yeraḥme’Al son of the sovereign and Serayahu son of Azri’Al and Shelemyahu son of Aḇde’Al, to seize Baruḵ the scribe and Yirmeyahu the naḇi, but (YAHUAH) HWHY had hid them.
And after the sovereign had burned the scroll with the words which Baruḵ had written from the mouth of Yirmeyahu, the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu, saying,
“Take another scroll and write on it all the former words that were in the first scroll which Yehoyaqim the sovereign of Yahuḏah has burned,

and say to Yehoyaqim sovereign of Yahuḏah, ‘Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, “You have burned this scroll, saying, ‘Why have you written in it that the sovereign of Baḇel is certainly coming to destroy this land and cause man and beast to cease from here?’ ”

‘Therefore thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY concerning Yehoyaqim sovereign of Yahuḏah, “He shall have no one to sit on the throne of Dawiḏ and his dead body is to be thrown out, to the heat of the day and the frost of the night.

“And I shall punish him and his seed and his servants for their crookedness. And I shall bring on them and on the inhabitants of Yerushalayim and on the men of Yahuḏah all the evil I have spoken against them. But they did not listen.” ’ ”

So Yirmeyahu took another scroll and gave it to Baruḵ the scribe, son of Nĕriyahu, who wrote on it from the mouth of Yirmeyahu all the words of the book which Yehoyaqim sovereign of Yahuḏah had burned in the fire. And many similar words were added to them.
And Sovereign Tsiḏqiyahu son of Yoshiyahu reigned instead of Konyahu son of Yehoyaqim, whom Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel set up to reign in the land of Yahuḏah.
But neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land had listened to the words of (YAHUAH) HWHY which He spoke by the naḇi Yirmeyahu.
And Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign sent Yehuḵal son of Shelemyah and the kohen, Tsephanyahu son of Ma‛asĕyah, to the naḇi Yirmeyahu, saying, “Please pray to (YAHUAH) HWHY our Aluahim for us.”
Now Yirmeyahu was still coming and going among the people, for they had not yet put him in prison.
Meanwhile, Pharaoh’s army had set out from Mitsrayim. And when the Chaldeans who were besieging Yerushalayim heard news of them, they withdrew from Yerushalayim.
And the Word of (YAHUAH) HWHY came to Yirmeyahu the naḇi, saying,

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Say this to the sovereign of Yahuḏah, who sent you to Me to inquire of Me, “See, Pharaoh’s army which has come up to help you, shall turn back to Mitsrayim, to their own land.
“And the Chaldeans shall return and fight against this city and take it and burn it with fire.” ’
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘Do not deceive yourselves, saying, “The Chaldeans shall go away from us,” for they do not go.

‘For though you had smitten the entire army of the Chaldeans who are fighting against you and there remained only wounded men among them, they would get up, each man in his tent and burn the city with fire.’ ”
And it came to be, when the army of the Chaldeans left the siege of Yerushalayim for fear of Pharaoh’s army,
that Yirmeyahu went out of Yerushalayim to go into the land of Binyamin to receive his portion there in the midst of the people.

And it came to be, as he was in the gate of Binyamin, a master of the guard was there whose name was Yiriyah son of Shelemyah, son of Ḥananyah. And he seized Yirmeyahu the naḇi, saying, “You are deserting to the Chaldeans!”
But Yirmeyahu said, “It is a lie! I am not deserting to the Chaldeans.” But he did not listen to him and Yiriyah seized Yirmeyahu and brought him to the heads.
And the heads were wroth with Yirmeyahu and smote him and put him in prison in the house of Yehonathan the scribe. For they had made that for a prison.
When Yirmeyahu had entered into the dungeon and into the cells, then Yirmeyahu remained there many days.

And Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign sent and took him out. And the sovereign asked him secretly in his house and said, “Is there any word from (YAHUAH) HWHY?” And Yirmeyahu said, “There is.” And he said, “You are given into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel!”
And Yirmeyahu said to Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign, “What have I sinned against you and against your servants and against this people, that you have put me into prison?
“Where are your neḇi'im who prophesied to you, saying, ‘The sovereign of Baḇel is not coming against you or against this land?’
“But now, please hear, O my master the sovereign. Please, let my petition be accepted before you and do not make me return to the house of Yehonathan the scribe, lest I die there.”

Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign then gave orders for Yirmeyahu to be placed in the court of the guard and that they should give him daily a piece of bread from the bakers’ street, until all the bread in the city was gone. So Yirmeyahu remained in the court of the guard.
And Shephatyah son of Mattan and Geḏalyahu son of Pashhur and Yuḵal son of Shelemyahu and Pashhur son of Malkiyah heard the words which Yirmeyahu had spoken to all the people, saying,

“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘He who remains in this city shall die by the sword, by scarcity of food and by pestilence, but whoever goes over to the Chaldeans shall live. And his life shall be as a prize to him and he shall live.’
“Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, ‘This city is certainly given into the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel’s army and he shall take it.’ ”

Then the heads said to the sovereign, “Please, let this man be put to death, because he is weakening the hands of the men of battle who are left in this city and the hands of all the people, by speaking such words to them. For this man does not seek the shalom of this people, but the evil.”
And Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign said, “Look, he is in your hand. For the sovereign is unable to do any matter against you.”

And they took Yirmeyahu and threw him into the dungeon of Malkiyahu the sovereign’s son, which was in the court of the guard and they let Yirmeyahu down with ropes. And in the dungeon there was no water, but mud. So Yirmeyahu sank in the mud.
And Eḇeḏ-Meleḵ the Kushite, one of the eunuchs, who was in the sovereign’s house, heard that they had put Yirmeyahu in the dungeon. And the sovereign was sitting at the Gate of Binyamin,
And Eḇeḏ-Meleḵ came from the sovereign’s house and spoke to the sovereign, saying,

“My master the sovereign, these men have done evil in all that they have done to Yirmeyahu the naḇi, whom they have thrown into the dungeon and he is likely to die from hunger in the place where he is, for there is no more bread in the city.”
And the sovereign commanded EḇeḏMeleḵ the Kushite, saying, “Take thirty men from here with you and lift Yirmeyahu the naḇi out of the dungeon before he dies.”
So Eḇeḏ-Meleḵ took the men with him and went into the house of the sovereign under the treasury and took old clothes and old rags from there and let them down by ropes into the dungeon to Yirmeyahu.
And Eḇeḏ-Meleḵ the Kushite said to Yirmeyahu, “Please put these old clothes and rags under your armpits, under the ropes.” And Yirmeyahu did so,
and they pulled Yirmeyahu up with ropes and lifted him out of the dungeon. And Yirmeyahu remained in the court of the guard.

Then Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign sent and had Yirmeyahu the naḇi brought to him at the third entrance of the House of (YAHUAH) HWHY. And the sovereign said to Yirmeyahu, “I am asking you a matter. Do not hide a matter from me.”
And Yirmeyahu said to Tsiḏqiyahu, “If I declare it to you, shall you not put me to death? And if I give you advice, you shall not listen to me.”

But Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign swore secretly to Yirmeyahu, saying, “As (YAHUAH) HWHY lives, who made us this life, I do not put you to death, nor do I give you into the hand of these men who seek your life.”

And Yirmeyahu said to Tsiḏqiyahu, “Thus said (YAHUAH) HWHY, the Aluahim of hosts, the Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘If you do indeed surrender to the heads of the sovereign of Baḇel, then your being shall live and this city not be burned with fire. And you and your house shall live.

‘But if you do not surrender to the heads of the sovereign of Baḇel, then this city shall be given into the hand of the Chaldeans and they shall burn it with fire and you shall not escape from their hand.’ ”
Then Tsiḏqiyahu the sovereign said to Yirmeyahu, “I am afraid of the Yahuḏim who have gone over to the Chaldeans, lest they give me into their hand and they maltreat me.”
But Yirmeyahu said, “They shall not hand you over. Please, obey the voice of (YAHUAH) HWHY which I speak to you and let it be well with you and your life be spared.
“But if you refuse to surrender, this is the word that (YAHUAH) HWHY has shown me:

‘Then see, all the women who are left in the sovereign of Yahuḏah’s house shall be surrendered to the heads of the sovereign of Baḇel and see, they are saying, “Your close friends have set upon you and prevailed against you; your feet have sunk in the mire; they have turned away again.”

‘And they are going to surrender all your wives and children to the Chaldeans and you yourself shall not escape from their hand, but be taken by the hand of the sovereign of Baḇel and this city shall be burned with fire.’ ”
Then Tsiḏqiyahu said to Yirmeyahu, “Let no one know of these words and you shall not die.

“But if the heads hear that I have spoken with you and they come to you and say to you, ‘Declare to us now what you have said to the sovereign and also what the sovereign said to you. Do not hide it from us and we do not put you to death,’
then you shall say to them, ‘I presented my petition before the sovereign, not to send me back to the house of Yonathan to die there.’ ”
And all the heads came to Yirmeyahu and asked him. And he informed them according to all these words that the sovereign had commanded. And they said no more to him, for the matter was not heard.
So Yirmeyahu remained in the court of the guard until the day that Yerushalayim was taken and he was there when Yerushalayim was taken.
In the ninth year of Tsiḏqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah, in the tenth month, Neḇuḵaḏretstsar sovereign of Baḇel and all his army came against Yerushalayim and besieged it.
In the Aleventh year of Tsiḏqiyahu, in the fourth month, on the ninth day of the month, the city was broken into.
